# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image – https://hub.docker.com/r/python/
FROM python:3-slim

# Set environment variables (replace with your own) setenv(key, value). For example setting the default timezone to UTC in Ubuntu containers via this line of code and then use it when creating other Dockerfiles or images within a container. — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/setenv/
ENV TZ='UTC'
# Set an environment variable on the fly during runtime (useful for setting default values) setx(). For example, this line sets 'DEBIAN_FRONTEND', which is used by apt to prevent user interaction. — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/setx/.
RUN setx DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive && \
    apt-get update -yqq --no-install-recommends > /dev/null; echo "$TZ" > /etc/timezone && dpkg-reconfigure -f timezone-data locales all — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
# Install Python and tools needed for the project (replace with your own) add(). This line adds a new user to docker group, so you can use sudo commands inside this container without needing root permissions anymore on host machine or vice versa — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/add/.
RUN apt-get install -yqq --no-install-recommends \
    curl \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* > /dev/null; echo "Installed Python and tools needed for the project" — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
# Set environment variable on runtime, which can be used to set default values in your Dockerfile or images within a container (for example setting PYTHONPATH). For instance: ENV SOME_VAR="Hello World". — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#env
ENV MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD rootpassword \ # set your password here, or use an environment variable if you have one (replace 'rootpassword' with the name of a secret in Kubernetes for example) — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
ENV MYSQL_DATABASE mydatabase   \ # set your database here, or use an environment variable if you have one (replace 'mydatabase' with the name of a secret in Kubernetes for example) — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
ENV MYSQL_USER mysqluser    \   # Set user and password here, or use an environment variable if you have one (replace 'mysqluser' with the name of a secret in Kubernetes for example) — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
ENV MYSQL_PASSWORD mysqlpassword \   # Set user and password here, or use an environment variable if you have one (replace 'mysqlpassword' with the name of a secret in Kubernetes for example) — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
# Install MySQL server using apt-get install(...) command inside Dockerfile, and also copy mysql files to container’s /etc directory (replace 'rootpassword' with the name of a secret in Kubernetes for example) — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
RUN apt-get update -yqq --no-install-recommends && \
    DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive dpkg-reconfigure -f noninteractive tzdata lvm2 mysql-server > /dev/null; echo "Installed MySQL server" — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34819065 and \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -yqq curl wget unzip zip tar xz-utils python3-pip build-essential libpq-dev nodejs > /dev/null; echo "Installed tools" — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
# COPY (copy files from local directory to the Docker image) copy() command inside this block, copies your project’s source code and its dependencies into a container's /app folder on build time when you run docker-build in development mode. — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#copy
COPY . ./
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt > /dev/null; echo "Installed Python dependencies" — https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27069134
EXPOSE $PORT # (expose ports in the Dockerfile) expose() command inside this block, exposes port(s), which means your container can be reached by other containers on a specific IP address and has access to certain exposed services. — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#expose
CMD ["python3", "./app.py"] # (run command when Docker image is run) CMD () runs the specified program or command as a container’s entry point, usually an executable file for non-executable files like python scripts and executables in Windows systems — https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#cmd
